args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all businesses with attributes
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

wifi_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi and wifi != "u'no'" and 'no' not in str(wifi).lower():
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(wifi_businesses))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all businesses with attributes
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

wifi_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi and wifi != "u'no'" and 'no' not in str(wifi).lower():
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(wifi_businesses))

args.code:
import json

# Access the businesses data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

wifi_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi and ('free' in str(wifi) or 'paid' in str(wifi)):
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(wifi_businesses))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json

# Access the businesses data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

wifi_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi and ('free' in str(wifi) or 'paid' in str(wifi)):
            wifi_businesses.append(biz)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(wifi_businesses))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all WiFi businesses from the query result
wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Extract state from description and business_id
wifi_biz_data = []
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    business_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter codes like CA, MO, FL, LA, IL)
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*\d{5}', description)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
    else:
        state = 'Unknown'
    
    wifi_biz_data.append({
        'business_id': business_id,
        'name': biz.get('name'),
        'state': state,
        'description': description
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(wifi_biz_data))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all WiFi businesses from the query result
wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Extract state from description and business_id
wifi_biz_data = []
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    business_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter codes like CA, MO, FL, LA, IL)
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*\d{5}', description)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
    else:
        state = 'Unknown'
    
    wifi_biz_data.append({
        'business_id': business_id,
        'name': biz.get('name'),
        'state': state,
        'description': description
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(wifi_biz_data))

import re

# Get all WiFi businesses from the query result
wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Extract state from description and business_id
wifi_biz_data = []
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    business_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter codes like CA, MO, FL, LA, IL)
    # Pattern: comma, optional spaces, 2 capital letters, then comma or space
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\s]', description)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
    else:
        state = 'Unknown'
    
    wifi_biz_data.append({
        'business_id': business_id,
        'name': biz.get('name'),
        'state': state,
        'description': description
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(wifi_biz_data))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.query: SELECT business_ref, AVG(CAST(rating AS INTEGER)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review WHERE business_ref IN ('businessref_54', 'businessref_26') GROUP BY business_ref;

exec_args.sql: SELECT business_ref, AVG(CAST(rating AS INTEGER)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review WHERE business_ref IN ('businessref_54', 'businessref_26') GROUP BY business_ref;

result.preview: {"state_counts": {"MO": 1, "FL": 2, "LA": 1, "IL": 1}, "state_businesses": {"MO": ["businessid_64"], "FL": ["businessid_54", "businessid_26"], "LA": ["businessid_91"], "IL": ["businessid_93"]}}

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all WiFi businesses
wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Extract state from each business
state_counts = {}
state_businesses = {}

for biz in wifi_businesses:
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    business_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter codes like CA, MO, FL, LA, IL)
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\s]', description)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
    else:
        state = 'Unknown'
    
    # Count businesses by state
    state_counts[state] = state_counts.get(state, 0) + 1
    if state not in state_businesses:
        state_businesses[state] = []
    state_businesses[state].append(business_id)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'state_counts': state_counts, 'state_businesses': state_businesses}))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all WiFi businesses
wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Extract state from each business
state_counts = {}
state_businesses = {}

for biz in wifi_businesses:
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    business_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract state (2-letter codes like CA, MO, FL, LA, IL)
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\s]', description)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
    else:
        state = 'Unknown'
    
    # Count businesses by state
    state_counts[state] = state_counts.get(state, 0) + 1
    if state not in state_businesses:
        state_businesses[state] = []
    state_businesses[state].append(business_id)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'state_counts': state_counts, 'state_businesses': state_businesses}))

args.code:
import json

# Get review ratings for Florida WiFi businesses
fl_ratings = []
for row in locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']:
    avg_rating = float(row['avg_rating'])
    review_count = int(row['review_count'])
    fl_ratings.append((avg_rating, review_count))

# Calculate weighted average
weighted_sum = sum(rating * count for rating, count in fl_ratings)
total_reviews = sum(count for _, count in fl_ratings)
avg_rating_fl = weighted_sum / total_reviews

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'state': 'FL',
    'wifi_business_count': len(fl_ratings),
    'avg_rating': round(avg_rating_fl, 2),
    'total_reviews': total_reviews
}))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re

wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']
business_ids = [biz['business_id'] for biz in wifi_businesses]

# Map business_id to state
biz_to_state = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\s]', description)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    biz_to_state[business_id] = state

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'business_ids': business_ids, 'biz_to_state': biz_to_state}))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']
business_ids = [biz['business_id'] for biz in wifi_businesses]

# Map business_id to state
biz_to_state = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\s]', description)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    biz_to_state[business_id] = state

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'business_ids': business_ids, 'biz_to_state': biz_to_state}))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.query:
SELECT 
  r.business_ref, 
  AVG(CAST(r.rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, 
  COUNT(*) as review_count
FROM review r 
WHERE r.business_ref IN ('businessref_64', 'businessref_54', 'businessref_91', 'businessref_93', 'businessref_26')
GROUP BY r.business_ref;

exec_args.sql:
SELECT 
  r.business_ref, 
  AVG(CAST(r.rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, 
  COUNT(*) as review_count
FROM review r 
WHERE r.business_ref IN ('businessref_64', 'businessref_54', 'businessref_91', 'businessref_93', 'businessref_26')
GROUP BY r.business_ref;

result.preview: {"state_with_most_wifi": "FL", "wifi_business_count": 2, "average_rating": 2.24, "state_counts": {"MO": 1, "FL": 2, "LA": 1, "IL": 1}}

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all businesses with WiFi
wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Get review data
review_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:60']

# Map states to businesses
state_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\s]', description)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    if state not in state_biz_map:
        state_biz_map[state] = []
    
    business_ref = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    state_biz_map[state].append({
        'business_id': business_id,
        'business_ref': business_ref,
        'name': biz.get('name')
    })

# Count WiFi businesses per state
state_counts = {state: len(businesses) for state, businesses in state_biz_map.items()}

# Find state with most WiFi businesses
max_state = max(state_counts, key=state_counts.get)
max_count = state_counts[max_state]

# Get review data for the max state businesses
max_state_businesses = state_biz_map[max_state]
max_state_refs = [biz['business_ref'] for biz in max_state_businesses]

# Calculate weighted average rating for the max state
total_rating = 0
total_reviews = 0

for row in review_data:
    business_ref = row['business_ref']
    if business_ref in max_state_refs:
        avg_rating = float(row['avg_rating'])
        review_count = int(row['review_count'])
        total_rating += avg_rating * review_count
        total_reviews += review_count

if total_reviews > 0:
    avg_rating = total_rating / total_reviews
else:
    avg_rating = 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'state_with_most_wifi': max_state,
    'wifi_business_count': max_count,
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
    'state_counts': state_counts
}))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

wifi_businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:38']

# Get review data
review_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:60']

# Map states to businesses
state_biz_map = {}
for biz in wifi_businesses:
    business_id = biz['business_id']
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\s]', description)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else 'Unknown'
    
    if state not in state_biz_map:
        state_biz_map[state] = []
    
    business_ref = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    state_biz_map[state].append({
        'business_id': business_id,
        'business_ref': business_ref,
        'name': biz.get('name')
    })

# Count WiFi businesses per state
state_counts = {state: len(businesses) for state, businesses in state_biz_map.items()}

# Find state with most WiFi businesses
max_state = max(state_counts, key=state_counts.get)
max_count = state_counts[max_state]

# Get review data for the max state businesses
max_state_businesses = state_biz_map[max_state]
max_state_refs = [biz['business_ref'] for biz in max_state_businesses]

# Calculate weighted average rating for the max state
total_rating = 0
total_reviews = 0

for row in review_data:
    business_ref = row['business_ref']
    if business_ref in max_state_refs:
        avg_rating = float(row['avg_rating'])
        review_count = int(row['review_count'])
        total_rating += avg_rating * review_count
        total_reviews += review_count

if total_reviews > 0:
    avg_rating = total_rating / total_reviews
else:
    avg_rating = 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'state_with_most_wifi': max_state,
    'wifi_business_count': max_count,
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
    'state_counts': state_counts
}))

var_functions.query_db:32.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
